name: Phil Bowler
abstract: Phillip Charles Bowler (born March 2, 1948, New York City) is an American jazz double-bassist and radio host.

concerts.label: Kool Jazz Festival: Young New Orleans

about_quotes.speaker: Jackie McLean
about_quotes.evidence: I play with my son, René, and my rhythm section that I usually keep, which has Eric McPherson, Alan Palmer, and Phil Bowler, René and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jackie McLean — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Branford Marsalis
about_quotes.evidence: And we had this bass player, Phil Bowler... We started calling him 'Sua vain.'
about_quotes.doc_title: Branford Marsalis — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Delfeayo Marsalis
about_quotes.evidence: And Phil Bowler. He had a number of bassists... The bass sounded – it was an electric signal, so that they could make it sound present, very present, but it didn’t fit the sound spectrum of the rest of the band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Delfeayo Marsalis — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
